flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.

tokens.range: five
a yard (as inclosed by a fence); also a hamlet (as similarly surrounded with walls)
Etam, a place in Palestine
Ajin, the name (thus simply) of two places in Palestine
a city (a place guarded by waking or a watch) in the widest sense (even of a mere encampment or post)
Ashan, a place in Palestine
Rimmon, the name of a Syrian deity, also of five places in Palestine
Token, a place in Palestine
a day (as the warm hours), whether literal (from sunrise to sunset, or from one sunset to the next), or figurative (a space of time defined by an associated term), (often used adverb)
properly, to sit down (specifically as judge. in ambush, in quiet); by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry
to strike (lightly or severely, literally or figuratively)
Amalek, a descendant of Esau; also his posterity and their country
deliverance; concretely, an escaped portion
a remainder or residual (surviving, final) portion
